skill.name: Startup Analyst
skill.description: Expert startup business analyst for market sizing, financial modeling, competitive analysis, and strategic planning for early-stage companies.
skill.summary: Provides data-driven business analysis for pre-seed to Series A startups including TAM/SAM/SOM calculations, unit economics, and fundraising preparation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis correctly identified zero security risk. The skill uses web search for market data research, which is a standard AI assistant capability and not a security concern.

content.user_title: Analyze startup opportunities and build investor-ready financial models
content.value_statement: First-time founders struggle with market sizing and financial projections that investors trust. This skill delivers data-driven analysis using proven frameworks to build credible business cases for fundraising.
actual_capabilities: Calculate TAM/SAM/SOM using bottom-up and top-down methodologies with credible data sources
Build 3-5 year cohort-based financial models with scenario planning
Analyze unit economics including CAC, LTV, payback period, and burn multiple
Map competitive landscapes using Porter's Five Forces and positioning frameworks
Create hiring plans and compensation benchmarks by startup stage
Define and track KPIs specific to SaaS, marketplace, consumer, and B2B models
limitations: Cannot access private company data or internal financial records
Market data from web search may have publication date lag
Financial projections are estimates based on stated assumptions, not guarantees
Cannot execute actual financial modeling software or spreadsheet applications

use_cases.title: Fundraising Preparation
use_cases.description: Prepare investor-ready market sizing and financial projections for seed or Series A pitch decks with defensible assumptions and benchmark comparisons.
use_cases.target_user: Pre-seed to Series A founders raising capital

use_cases.title: Strategic Planning
use_cases.description: Evaluate go-to-market strategies, pricing models, and customer segmentation with data-driven recommendations for resource allocation.
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders and early employees making strategic decisions

use_cases.title: Board Reporting
use_cases.description: Generate structured analysis of key metrics, competitive positioning, and milestone progress for board meetings and investor updates.
use_cases.target_user: CEOs and CFOs preparing board materials

prompt_templates.title: Quick Market Sizing
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate the TAM, SAM, and SOM for a [business type] targeting [customer segment] in [geographic market]. Use bottom-up methodology with credible data sources and show all calculations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Early founder validating market opportunity for pitch deck

prompt_templates.title: Unit Economics Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the unit economics for my [SaaS/marketplace/consumer] business. Current metrics: CAC $[X], MRR $[Y], churn [Z]%. Calculate LTV, payback period, and compare to industry benchmarks for [stage] companies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Seed-stage founder assessing business model health

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Positioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the competitive landscape for [product category]. Identify top 5 competitors, analyze their positioning using a 2x2 matrix, and recommend differentiation strategies based on Blue Ocean framework.

prompt_templates.title: Financial Model Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my 3-year financial model assumptions for a [business type] with current $[X] MRR. Evaluate growth rate, margin projections, and hiring plan against benchmarks for companies at my stage raising [round size]. Flag unrealistic assumptions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Series A founder stress-testing financials before fundraising

output_examples.input: Calculate TAM for B2B SaaS project management tool targeting construction companies in the US
output_examples.output: TAM Analysis: Top-down approach estimates $2.4B (15% of $16B construction software market). Bottom-up approach: 734K US construction firms x $800/year ARPU = $587M. Value theory: 50K target firms x $1,200/year = $60M near-term SOM. Recommendation: Use $587M TAM for investor conversations with clear methodology disclosure.

output_examples.input: Analyze unit economics: CAC $2,500, ARPA $4,800/year, gross margin 75%, monthly churn 3%
output_examples.output: Unit Economics Summary: LTV = $4,800 / 0.03 x 0.75 = $120,000. LTV:CAC ratio = 48:1 (excellent, target is 3:1+). CAC payback = $2,500 / ($400 x 0.75) = 8.3 months (strong, target is <12 months). These metrics indicate efficient growth and strong unit economics for Series A investors.

best_practices: Always document assumptions explicitly and cite data sources with publication dates for credibility
Use multiple methodologies to triangulate market size and validate findings against benchmarks
Present findings in structured tables with clear headers, formulas shown, and actionable next steps
anti_patterns: Relying solely on top-down market research reports without bottom-up validation
Using overly optimistic growth assumptions without benchmark comparison or scenario analysis
Presenting analysis without acknowledging data limitations or competitive context

faq.question: What company stages does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on pre-seed through Series A startups. Each stage has different investor expectations and metric benchmarks that the analysis accounts for.

faq.question: Can this skill create actual spreadsheet financial models?
faq.answer: No, it cannot create Excel or Google Sheets files directly. It provides the analysis, assumptions, and formulas you need to build models in your preferred spreadsheet application.

faq.question: How current is the market data used in analysis?
faq.answer: The skill uses web search to find current market data. Data sources are always cited with publication dates so you can assess recency and relevance.

faq.question: What business models does this skill cover?
faq.answer: Primary focus on SaaS, marketplace, consumer, and B2B business models. Each has specific metrics, benchmarks, and analytical frameworks applied appropriately.

faq.question: Can this skill help with pitch deck creation?
faq.answer: It provides the analytical content for market sizing, financial projections, and competitive analysis sections. You will need to integrate this content into your pitch deck design tool.

faq.question: Does this skill replace a financial analyst or consultant?
faq.answer: It provides framework-based analysis and data-driven insights but does not replace human judgment for complex strategic decisions or regulatory compliance requirements.
